[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] zip_strict del demo lanza ValueError si len(a)!=len(b), equivalente pedagógico a strict=True. Detecta desalineación de ids/regiones antes de procesar el lote.

exercises.code:
lines = ["r1", "r2", "", "r3"]
i = 0
out = []

    line = lines[i]
    i += 1
    if line == "":
        break
    out.append(line)
print(out)

exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] while con índice acumula hasta centinela vacío y break, sin incluir el centinela. Resultado ['r1','r2']; r3 queda fuera como en el buffer END del demo.

exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] while cuando no sabes de antemano el fin exacto; la variable de control se incrementa dentro. Al salir intentos==MAX y se imprime done; olvidar el += causa loop infinito.

exercises.code:
cola = ["job1", "job2", "job3"]
while cola:
    job = cola.pop(0)
    print(job)
    if job == "job2":
        print("PAUSE")
        break
print("restante", cola)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T2-A-E3
exercises.answer:
cola = ["job1", "job2", "job3"]
while cola:
    job = cola.pop(0)
    print(job)
    if job == "job2":
        print("PAUSE")
        break
print("restante", cola)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Cola con while cola y pop(0); break en job2 deja ['job3'] restante. break sale del bucle actual de inmediato, patrón del paquete de terminación.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] continue salta al siguiente ciclo: filas vacías de intake no se procesan. Solo Lima y Cusco; el demo lines usa continue en not ln.strip().

exercises.code:
codes = [200, 200, 500, 200]
n_ok = 0
for c in codes:
    if c >= 500:
        print("STOP")
        break
    print("ok")
    n_ok += 1
print("n_ok", n_ok)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T2-B-E2
exercises.answer:
codes = [200, 200, 500, 200]
n_ok = 0
for c in codes:
    if c >= 500:
        print("STOP")
        break
    print("ok")
    n_ok += 1
print("n_ok", n_ok)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] break ante código fatal >=500 no procesa el 200 final; n_ok queda en 2. El demo para en ERROR y deja kept sin el resto del buffer.

exercises.code:
buf = ["a", "b", "END"]
i = 0
out = []
while True:
    if i > 10:
        raise RuntimeError("guard")
    item = buf[i]
    i += 1
    if item == "END":
        break
    out.append(item)
print(out)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T2-B-E3
exercises.answer:
buf = ["a", "b", "END"]
i = 0
out = []
while True:
    if i > 10:
        raise RuntimeError("guard")
    item = buf[i]
    i += 1
    if item == "END":
        break
    out.append(item)
print(out)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] while True es válido con break en centinela END y guardrail i>10. No proceso END como dato; out=['a','b'] como el demo de buffer.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Contadores por status del gate: n_accept, n_reject y n_total del lote. Demo counts con accept/reject/review; aquí 2,1,3.

exercises.code:
def tasa_reject(sts):
    n_total = len(sts)
    if n_total == 0:
        return None
    n_reject = sum(1 for s in sts if s == "reject")
    return n_reject / n_total
print(round(tasa_reject(["accept", "reject", "accept"]), 4))
print(tasa_reject([]))
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T3-A-E2
exercises.answer:
def tasa_reject(sts):
    n_total = len(sts)
    if n_total == 0:
        return None
    n_reject = sum(1 for s in sts if s == "reject")
    return n_reject / n_total
print(round(tasa_reject(["accept", "reject", "accept"]), 4))
print(tasa_reject([]))
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] tasa_reject = n_reject/n_total; denominador es n_total de registros procesados. Lista vacía devuelve None para evitar ZeroDivisionError, como en el demo.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Búsqueda del primer review con enumerate + break; si no hay, -1. No uso index(); trazo la búsqueda manual del paquete de contadores.

exercises.code:
rows = [{"id": "C1", "status": "accept"}, {"id": "C2", "status": "reject"}, {"id": "C3", "status": "accept"}, {"id": "C4", "status": "reject"}]
by = {r["id"]: r["status"] for r in rows}
rejects = [i for i, st in by.items() if st == "reject"]
tasa = len(rejects) / len(rows)
print(by["C2"], rejects, tasa)
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T3-B-E3
exercises.answer:
rows = [{"id": "C1", "status": "accept"}, {"id": "C2", "status": "reject"}, {"id": "C3", "status": "accept"}, {"id": "C4", "status": "reject"}]
by = {r["id"]: r["status"] for r in rows}
rejects = [i for i, st in by.items() if st == "reject"]
tasa = len(rejects) / len(rows)
print(by["C2"], rejects, tasa)
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Dict comprehension id→status y lista de rejects para tasa 0.5. El demo calcula n_rej/len(rows) con comprehension de reject_ids.

[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] TRACE por registro con copia del dict de contadores para ver el crecimiento. Igual que print i flag n_ok del demo de trazado de estado.
[D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Un for es O(n) lineal; doble for anidado es O(n²) cuadrático. Para n=5: linear 5 y quad 25 como en el demo steps_linear/steps_quad.

for i in range(len(data)):  # 0..n-1, sin off-by-one
    print(data[i])
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T4-B-E2
exercises.answer:
data = ["r0", "r1", "r2"]
for i in range(len(data)):  # 0..n-1, sin off-by-one
    print(data[i])
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Off-by-one: range(1, len+1) provoca IndexError; range(len(data)) es 0..n-1. El demo muestra range(1, len) saltando el índice 0 como error clásico.

exercises.code:
sts = ["reject", "accept", "reject", "reject", "accept"]
n_reject = sum(1 for s in sts if s == "reject")
n = len(sts)
tasa = n_reject / n
print("n_reject", n_reject, "tasa", tasa)
print("O(n) basta para la tasa: no necesitas pares reject-reject O(n²); denominador es n_total")
# D2-Explorer: packet-only re-walk S04 S04-T4-B-E3
exercises.answer:
sts = ["reject", "accept", "reject", "reject", "accept"]
n_reject = sum(1 for s in sts if s == "reject")
n = len(sts)
tasa = n_reject / n
print("n_reject", n_reject, "tasa", tasa)
print("O(n) basta para la tasa: no necesitas pares reject-reject O(n²); denominador es n_total")
exercises.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second full walk from landing] Para la tasa de reject basta un conteo O(n); el doble for de pares es ingenua. tasa = n_reject/n_total de registros procesados, no combinar pares.

selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] list(range(3)) produce [0,1,2] porque el stop es exclusivo. La teoría de for/range lo muestra junto a lotes de intake.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Empareja solo (1,10) y (2,20); el 3 se pierde en silencio
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] zip sin strict se detiene en la secuencia más corta y descarta el resto en silencio. Por eso el paquete enseña zip_strict cuando ids y regiones deben alinearse.
[D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] La tasa de reject del gate usa denominador n_total de registros procesados. No solo n_accept ni un 100 fijo; demo tasa_err = counts[reject]/n.

selfcheck.chosen_text: Salta al siguiente ciclo del bucle
selfcheck.justification_from_packet: [D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] continue salta al siguiente ciclo sin ejecutar el resto del cuerpo del for. En intake: continue para filas vacías; break para centinela fatal.
[D2-Explorer second consecutive agentic run] Doble for anidado sobre n elementos es aproximadamente O(n²) cuadrático. Un solo for es O(n); el demo cuenta steps_quad = n*n.
